glossary.def: A self-contained, versioned, serializable representation of just a model's forward computation and its trained weights, with the training scaffolding (autograd, optimizer state, Python imports) stripped away. Unlike a live notebook object it is portable, frozen, and optimizable — the thing deployment actually ships.

The two ways to recover a static computational graph from eager Python. Tracing runs one example and records the operations it executed (fast, but bakes in that input's control flow); scripting parses the source and compiles loops and branches into the graph (preserves dynamism over a typed subset of the language).

A framework-agnostic graph format plus a versioned standard operator set that lets a model trained in one framework run on runtimes written for another. It turns an F×R matrix of bespoke converters into F+R hub-and-spoke connectors and is the canonical route for cross-framework and browser deployment.

The version of the ONNX standard operator set a model is exported against. It is the portability contract: a model built for a given opset produces identical results on any runtime that implements that opset, which is what makes ONNX truly framework-neutral.

PyTorch's native artifact paths for running a model without Python. TorchScript (now in maintenance) captures the model into an IR for the libtorch C++ runtime; torch.export is its successor, producing an ahead-of-time ExportedProgram (FX graph) that feeds torch.compile and the ExecuTorch edge runtime.

TensorFlow's serving artifacts. SavedModel is the complete language-neutral graph-plus-variables directory that TF Serving and TensorFlow.js load; TFLite (LiteRT) is the edge sibling — a compact, mmap-able FlatBuffer, usually int8/float16 quantized, built for phones, microcontrollers, and embedded accelerators.

A serving technique that holds incoming requests for a few milliseconds and runs them through the model together, exploiting the fact that a GPU processes a batch of N almost as fast as a single item. It trades a little tail latency for a large throughput gain and is the central lever of high-utilization inference servers.

NVIDIA's framework-agnostic serving system that hosts models in many backends at once (TensorRT, ONNX Runtime, PyTorch, TensorFlow, vLLM) behind one HTTP/gRPC API, adding concurrent model execution, dynamic batching, ensembles, and metrics. The default when a fleet must serve a zoo of models from different frameworks on shared GPUs.

A NumPy-compatible library of composable function transformations — jit (compile to XLA), grad (autodiff), vmap (auto-vectorization), pmap/shard_map (device parallelism) — that compose orthogonally, e.g. jit(grad(vmap(f))). Its purity requirement powers large-scale TPU training and routes deployment through StableHLO rather than ONNX.

refs.cite: Bai, J., Lu, F., Zhang, K. et al. (2019). ONNX: Open Neural Network Exchange.
Bradbury, J., Frostig, R., Hawkins, P. et al. (2018). JAX: composable transformations of Python+NumPy programs.
Google. TensorFlow Lite / LiteRT — On-Device Machine Learning.
Kwon, W., Li, Z., Zhuang, S. et al. (2023). Efficient Memory Management for LLM Serving with PagedAttention (vLLM).
